editionData.title: Daily Brief for April 29, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for April 29, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: UAE Says It Will Leave OPEC as Iran War Strains Oil Markets
headlines.summary: The Gulf government has long complained about the group’s quotas, which officials believe unfairly limited its exports. Its departure is expected to weaken OPEC’s influence.

headlines.title: Internet Restrictions Spur Russians to Openly Question Putin’s Moves
headlines.summary: From beauty influencers to the token political opposition, Russians are openly questioning President Vladimir V. Putin’s moves to hamstring access.

headlines.title: The Small U.K. Agency That’s a 911 for Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: At a military base outside Portsmouth, England, a 24-hour service monitors the Persian Gulf, the Red Sea and part of the Indian Ocean, responding to distress calls.

headlines.title: They Lost Their Homes in Cuba to the Communist Government. Will They Ever Get Them Back?
headlines.summary: With Cuba in dire economic crisis, people whose properties were seized by its government decades ago say it’s time to resolve thorny compensation claims.

headlines.title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.

headlines.title: US appeals court rejects Trump’s immigration detention policy
Trump says Iran requesting end to US blockade; Israel kills three medics
Former FBI director Comey again indicted by US Justice Department
King Charles calls for NATO unity, Ukraine support in US Congress speech
Northern Ireland makes arrest linked to suspected New IRA car bombing
Yomif Kejelcha broke the 2-hour marathon but got 2nd place. He's still happy
headlines.summary: Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha ran the London Marathon in under two hours, but he only got second place. He told NPR he hopes to run his next marathon a minute faster.

headlines.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
headlines.summary: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

headlines.title: Linda McMahon punches back at senators questioning Education Department cuts
headlines.summary: In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.

headlines.title: With no radical footprint, what drove suspect to try and assassinate Trump?
headlines.summary: An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.

headlines.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
headlines.summary: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

headlines.title: Kenya's farm industry praises duty-free access
headlines.summary: Stakeholders in Kenya's agricultural sector have welcomed China's zero-tariff policy, set to take effect on Friday, saying it will spur one of the country's key industries.

headlines.title: Ukraine marks 40 years of Chernobyl disaster
headlines.summary: SLAVUTYCH, Ukraine — People streamed into the central square of Slavutych in the early hours of Sunday, placing candles on a large radiation hazard symbol laid out on the ground as a midnight commemoration began for those killed in the Chernobyl disaster 40 years ago and the thousands who risked deadly radiation exposure to contain its aftermath.

headlines.title: US shooting bares security vulnerabilities
headlines.summary: WASHINGTON — The shooting on Saturday night of a Secret Service agent at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner raises questions yet again about security at a time of increased political violence.

headlines.title: China urges restraint on Middle East at UN
headlines.summary: China's permanent representative to the United Nations said the Middle East is at a "critical stage of transition from war to peace", as fresh violence in Gaza and Lebanon and a widening energy shock underscored the fragility of the situation.

headlines.title: Sino-European collaboration crucial for reshaping order
headlines.summary: A reconfigured model of global co-governance is absolutely needed, with China and Europe bearing important responsibilities to work together in upholding a rule-based international order, former Italian prime minister Enrico Letta said in Shanghai on Friday.

headlines.title: Rajnath Singh calls for unified SCO action against terrorism, warns against double standards
headlines.summary: Rajnath Singh urges the SCO to combat state-sponsored terrorism without double standards, emphasising India's firm resolve against such threats.

headlines.title: 44 illegal telecom hubs shut in 2 years: Centre tells Supreme Court
headlines.summary: Centre reports dismantling of 44 illegal telecom centres in two years, addressing cybercrime concerns in Supreme Court meeting.

headlines.title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
headlines.summary: Salim Dola, a key associate of Dawood Ibrahim, deported to India after arrest in Istanbul, facing interrogation by authorities.

headlines.title: Rahul meets Nicobarese community, promises to fight against infra project
headlines.summary: Rahul Gandhi visits Great Nicobar, meeting community leaders to address concerns over a controversial infrastructure project despite administrative hurdles.

headlines.title: ‘Just peace’ can’t be found by joining organisations that charge fees: UNGA President defends UN system
headlines.summary: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock asserts that 'just peace' isn't attainable through fee-based organizations, emphasizing UN's inclusive approach.

headlines.title: As Trump greets Charles, the White House calls them 'TWO KINGS'
Exclusive: US spy agencies examine how Iran would react to Trump declaring victory
US ambassador to Ukraine to step down amid stalled talks to end Ukraine war
Trump's DOJ indicts former FBI director James Comey over '86 47' post
